2,Promise.allSettled,"parallel execution, fault-tolerant, partial failure, settled promises","Executes multiple promises in parallel and waits for ALL of them to settle (fulfill or reject). 6,Event Loop Understanding,microtasks, macrotasks, event loop phases, queueMicrotask, execution order","Understanding the Node.js event loop phases and the difference between microtasks (Promise callbacks, queueMicrotask, process.nextTick) and macrotasks (setTimeout, setInterval, setImmediate, I/O).","Understand that microtasks (Promise.then, queueMicrotask) run before macrotasks (setTimeout). Use queueMicrotask for scheduling work that must complete before the next I/O. Keep microtask queues short to avoid starving I/O.","Don't create infinite microtask loops — they block the event loop and starve I/O and timers. 8,Writable Streams,"stream writing, drain event, backpressure, write buffer","Writing data to writable streams while respecting backpressure. The write() method returns false when the internal buffer is full, signaling you should wait for the 'drain' event before writing more.","Always check the return value of stream.write(). Wait for the 'drain' event when write() returns false. Use stream.end() to signal completion. Handle 'error' and 'finish' events.","Don't ignore the return value of write() — it causes unbounded memory growth. Don't call write() after end(). Don't forget to handle the 'error' event on writable streams.","import { createWriteStream, WriteStream } from ""fs""

11,AbortController,"cancellation, abort signal, request cancellation, timeout abort","AbortController provides a standard mechanism to cancel async operations like fetch requests, streams, and custom async work. 12,Worker Threads,"CPU-intensive, off-main-thread, parallel processing, worker_threads","Worker threads run JavaScript in parallel OS threads, enabling CPU-intensive operations without blocking the main event loop. Communication happens via message passing with structured clone or SharedArrayBuffer.","Use worker threads for CPU-heavy tasks (parsing, compression, crypto). Use workerData for initialization data. Transfer ArrayBuffers instead of copying. Pool workers for reuse.","Don't use worker threads for I/O-bound work — the event loop handles I/O efficiently. Don't share mutable state without SharedArrayBuffer and Atomics. Don't create a new worker per request — use a pool.","import {

21,process.nextTick vs setImmediate,microtask priority, event loop phases, nextTick, setImmediate","process.nextTick queues a callback in the microtask queue (runs before any I/O), while setImmediate queues in the check phase (runs after I/O). Understanding their execution order is critical for correct async behavior.","Use setImmediate when you want to yield to the event loop and allow I/O to proceed. Use process.nextTick sparingly for ensuring callbacks run before any I/O. Prefer queueMicrotask over process.nextTick for new code.","Don't use recursive process.nextTick — it starves I/O. Don't assume process.nextTick and setImmediate are interchangeable — they have different priorities. Don't overuse process.nextTick when setImmediate would suffice.","// GOOD: using setImmediate to yield to event loop
